_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The HD GHG Phase 3 rule creates **no direct registration or reporting obligation for freight forwarders, logistics operators, or fleet operators**. All registration, certification, and annual reporting obligations under the rule fall on **vehicle manufacturers and engine manufacturers** that must certify new heavy-duty highway vehicles and engines to EPA before sale under 40 CFR parts 1036 and 1037.

**Published (in force):** Manufacturers must obtain EPA certificates of conformity for each covered engine family and vehicle configuration beginning with MY 2027 production. Certification applications must describe, in detail, all components needed to charge, store energy, and transmit power for any vehicle using a rechargeable energy storage system — a requirement designed to define the scope of emission-related warranty coverage.

**Averaging, Banking, and Trading (ABT) reporting:** Manufacturers participating in the ABT programme must track and report credits generated and used within three weight-class averaging sets (light heavy-duty Class 2b–5; medium heavy-duty; heavy heavy-duty). Updated Phase 3 provisions include new provisions for cross-set credit transfer, which affect manufacturer compliance calculations but not forwarder obligations.

**Data fields the workspace should collect (indirect, for carrier due-diligence and scope-3 reporting purposes):** vehicle model year; gross vehicle weight rating (GVWR) class; powertrain type (ICE, hybrid, PHEV, BEV, FCEV); EPA certification status; estimated CO2 grams per ton-mile for road legs on US domestic lanes. These data points are not mandated by Phase 3 for the workspace, but may be required by shipper clients' own sustainability reporting frameworks (e.g., GHG Protocol scope-3 Category 4 upstream transportation) or future complementary EPA fleet-reporting rules.

**Registration scope:** Federal; applies to US-manufactured and imported heavy-duty vehicles sold for use on US highways. Relevant trade lanes for the workspace: US domestic road legs serving Americas freight corridors, including air-to-road intermodal transfers at major US gateway airports.

*Source: Greenhouse Gas Emissions Standards for Heavy-Duty Vehicles—Phase 3, EPA, 89 FR 29440, April 22, 2024. https://federalregister.gov/documents/2024/04/22/2024-06809/greenhouse-gas-emissions-standards-for-heavy-duty-vehicles-phase-3. Hogan Lovells, EPA finalizes new HD GHG Phase 3 emission standards, April 2024. https://www.hoganlovells.com/en/publications/epa-finalizes-new-heavy-duty-ghg-phase-3-emission-standards-for-model-years-2027-2032.*
